context.background: The Higgins Unity Framework (HUF) develops the Entropy-Invariant Time Transformer (EITT): Shannon entropy of compositional data is near-invariant under geometric-mean block decimation across temporal resolutions. Four empirical proofs validate this (European electricity prices 0.18%/341:1, EMBER monthly 1.02% mean, NGFS scenarios 1.8% geometric vs 14.2% arithmetic, CheMixHub 54-82% interior pass).
context.recent_synthesis: A shape/magnitude decomposition generalizes EITT: shape functionals (entropy, correlations, principal balances, affinity clusters) are invariant under EITT decimation; magnitude functionals (Aitchison variance, total variation) scale as 1/M. The Fisher-Rao vs Aitchison geometry tension dissolves because both agree infinitesimally at the Fréchet mean and EITT never leaves the infinitesimal regime.
context.novelty_claim_under_test: A five-object cluster (EITT + Tutte's theorem + Hanani-Tutte theorem + Penrose tilings + KPZ universality) organized under a single shape/magnitude decomposition is novel in the literature. Each pairwise connection to EITT may also be individually novel. We are testing both.

your_branch_assignment.scope: Tutte theorem + Hanani-Tutte theorem + their possible connections to compositional data analysis (CoDa), simplex geometry, sequential binary partitions (SBP), and entropy Taylor expansions
your_branch_assignment.why_claude: You have participated in developing the framework and understand the mathematical structure. You are well-positioned to judge whether a claimed combinatorial-CoDa connection is substantive or superficial. Your knowledge cutoff is late May 2025; any 2025+ papers you can access via web search should be checked but older references in your training are already known to you.

hypothesis_to_test.H1: Tutte's theorem (perfect matching existence) has not been explicitly connected to sequential binary partition (SBP) existence in the CoDa literature.
hypothesis_to_test.H2: Hanani-Tutte theorem (parity of crossings for planarity) has not been connected to parity cancellation in the Taylor expansion of Shannon entropy under compositional averaging.
hypothesis_to_test.H3: Graph-matching theoretic language is absent from the SBP theory of Egozcue & Pawlowsky-Glahn (2005 and later).

tasks.action: For each hit, extract title/authors/year/abstract. Classify as positive/near-miss/irrelevant.
tasks.positive_definition: Paper explicitly uses Tutte matching theorem in the context of compositional data analysis.

tasks.action: Same as T1. Filter for statistical/data-analysis context, exclude pure combinatorics papers about simplices of graphs.
Direct pairing test. Any hit is significant.
Any hit is highly significant — this is the most unlikely pairing.
Probe adjacent area. Even tangential hits are notable.
Tests whether anyone has used matching-theoretic language for SBP construction.
Alternative phrasing for parity cancellation in entropy expansions.

tasks.source: Egozcue & Pawlowsky-Glahn (2005) 'Groups of parts and their balances in compositional data analysis' or any subsequent SBP paper by this group
tasks.action: Scan for matching-theoretic language, graph-theoretic existence conditions, parity arguments, or Tutte/Hanani-Tutte citations. Report findings in 2-4 sentences.
tasks.access: If paywall blocks, use abstract + references section only.

tasks.action: Based on T1-T10 results, assess hypotheses H1, H2, H3 with confidence level (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW) and brief justification.
tasks.note: This is the synthesis step — use judgment, not just search returns.

required_fields.task_results: array of {task_id, query, n_hits, top_hits: [{title, authors, year, venue, relevance}], verdict: positive|near_miss|negative}
required_fields.surprise_findings: Anything unexpected discovered along the way
required_fields.recommended_next_searches: Queries that would tighten the verification further

verdict_definitions.positive: A paper explicitly makes the claimed connection. Novelty claim for that pairing is FALSIFIED. Cite the paper clearly so the framework can acknowledge the prior work.
verdict_definitions.near_miss: A paper is in adjacent territory but does not make the explicit connection. Note for the discussion section of any future paper. Novelty claim remains.
verdict_definitions.negative: No relevant hits. Novelty claim for that pairing is tentatively supported.

overlap_with_others.ChatGPT: ChatGPT is reviewing recent papers from Pawlowsky-Glahn / Egozcue / Tolosana-Delgado group (2020-2025) for any mention of matching theory, parity, aperiodic, universality. If they find Tutte/Hanani-Tutte mentions, flag it and reconcile.
overlap_with_others.Copilot: Copilot is running arXiv crossover searches. If they find a math.CO × stat.ME preprint with compositional content, that overlaps with your T6 and T7.

coordination_notes.escalate_if: Any task returns a strongly positive hit. Peter wants to know immediately so the framework can cite correctly rather than claim novelty incorrectly.

estimated_effort: 1-2 hours of focused search + 30 minutes synthesis
